mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or signal Rank IC drops below 0.01 during labor shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, or interaction term Rank IC remains below 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery phases, or mean forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero with p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained economic activity and wage growth, supporting consumer spending and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive relationship with 5-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds the 300 index threshold, aggressive monetary tightening expectations dominate market pricing, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 300, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and monthly retail sales growth confirm synchronized demand strength across production and consumption channels, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns, or the interaction effect Rank IC remains below 0.03 over rolling 3-year evaluation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and supply chain bottlenecks, triggering short-term risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, or mean forward returns remain positive with p < 0.05 significance.
